mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to discount equity cash flows and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp threshold, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, boosting corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening economic momentum and higher factory utilization, which translates into immediate positive earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 5-day forward returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to adopt a hawkish stance, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, which generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in over 60% of periods following CPIAUCSL acceleration, or the signal shows no predictive power when core inflation remains stable.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Exceptionally high real GDP growth signals an overheating economy that triggers anticipatory monetary tightening and profit-taking, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: High GDPC1 readings consistently coincide with positive 5-day forward returns, or the negative relationship disappears when market volatility remains below 15%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial output and payroll employment confirm a synchronized supply-side expansion, reducing recession probabilities and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform single-series models, or positive returns do not materialize when either series diverges from its 12-month trend.
